howItWorks.intro: This era calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Only earned runs count — runs from errors are excluded
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a good ERA?
faq.answer: In MLB: under 2.50 is elite, 2.50-3.50 is very good, 3.50-4.00 is above average, 4.00-4.50 is average, and above 5.00 is poor. In lower leagues, average ERAs tend to be higher.

faq.question: What is the difference between ERA and earned runs?
faq.answer: Earned runs are runs that score without the aid of fielding errors or passed balls. ERA normalizes this to a per-9-inning rate, allowing comparison between pitchers with different innings totals.

faq.question: What is the lowest ERA in MLB history?
faq.answer: The modern record (post-1900) is 0.96 by Dutch Leonard in 1914 (minimum 154 innings). Bob Gibson's 1.12 in 1968 is the most famous low ERA, which led to the lowering of the mound in 1969.

faq.question: Is the ERA Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the ERA Calculator?
sections.body: The ERA Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your earned runs, innings pitched, you get instant results including era, rating, earned runs per 9 ip. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting era right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your earned runs and need to find the right era. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
